CPTAC Case ID: Unique identifier for each case in the CPTAC database.
Tumor analyzed: Identifier for tumor samples analyzed, likely corresponding to CPTAC Case ID.
Normal FT analyzed: Identifier for normal fallopian tube samples analyzed, related to CPTAC Case ID.
Age in Months at Time of Tissue Procurem: Age of the patient in months at the time of tissue collection.
Jewish Religion/Heritage: Information on Jewish heritage or religion of the patient.
Site of Disease: Primary site of the disease in the patient.
Anatomic site of Tumor Specimen: Specific anatomic location of the tumor specimen.
Anatomic Lateral Position of Tumor Speci: Indicates the side of the tumor relative to the body midline.
Histological Subtype: Describes the specific type of cancer cell present in the tumor.
Method of Initial Pathologic Diagnosis: Details the surgical procedure used to diagnose the tumor.
Tumor Stage (Pathological) Ovary FIGO St: Represents the extent of cancer spread using the FIGO staging system.
Tumor Grade: Indicates the level of differentiation of cancer cells, with G3 being poorly differentiated.
Tumor Residual Disease (for the max diam: Describes the size of the largest remaining tumor nodule post-treatment.
History of Other Malignancy?: Indicates if the patient has a history of other cancers.
History of Systemic Chemotherapy?: Indicates if the patient has received chemotherapy.
History or Radiation Therapy for Any Pri: Indicates if the patient has received radiation therapy to the abdomen or pelvis.
History of Hormonal Therapy for Cancer w: Indicates if the patient has received hormonal therapy for cancer in the last five years.
History of Neo-Adjuvant Treatment to Tum: Indicates if neo-adjuvant treatment was applied to the tumor specimen (Yes/No).
Ischemia Time for Tumor Tissue: Duration in minutes that the tumor tissue was without blood supply before surgery.
